behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is in the diff-parsing/scoping function itself — does it extract only changed lines from a diff object? That is a single algorithmic boundary with no module handoff. REAL-INFRA: A fabricated diff string or object is sufficient; no real HTTP, OS, or subprocess involved. LLM-DEP: This is pre-LLM input preparation; LLM output plays no role. MULTI-STEP: Single function. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: The failure mode is not at a module boundary — it is entirely within whichever function slices diff hunks before passing them downstream.
behaviors.test_description: Feed a synthetic unified diff (containing both changed lines and unchanged context lines) to the input-scoping function. Assert the returned review payload contains exactly the added/removed lines from the diff hunks and does not contain lines outside those hunks from the original file content.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: 'Correct position in the diff view' is ultimately defined by what GitHub's API accepts and renders. The position integer is a GitHub-specific count of diff lines including context, not just changed lines. An in-process fake or a mocked HTTP client can validate what the code sends, but it cannot catch misunderstandings of GitHub's diff-position scheme (e.g., off-by-one in hunk parsing, using wrong field — `line` vs `position` vs `start_line`). The real failure mode only surfaces when GitHub rejects the request or renders the comment at the wrong line. MOCK-HIDE: A mock that returns 201 regardless of position value would always pass and hide the bug. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: cannot point to any evidence that an in-process fake faithfully enforces GitHub's position semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Push a small known diff to a real test GitHub repository, invoke the inline-comment posting function against that PR, then retrieve the resulting review comments via the GitHub REST API. Assert each comment's `path`, `position` (or `line`/`side`) fields match the expected locations in the diff, and that GitHub rendered them on the correct diff lines.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: The failure mode is that the LLM-based analyzer fails to recognise a real defect. No deterministic code path governs whether the model notices the bug; the outcome varies with model temperature, prompt version, and model weights. Running this 100 times with real LLM calls could yield different detection rates. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking the LLM with a hardcoded response trivially passes and tells you nothing about actual defect-detection quality. MULTI-STEP: Identification is a single analytical step, not a chain of agentic steps, so Workflow is not required. REJECTION-GATE for Integration: the behavior is explicitly about whether the model 'identifies' a defect — that is a quality claim about LLM output, not about module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Prepare a diff that contains a deliberately injected, clearly recognisable bug (e.g., an unchecked None dereference or an off-by-one boundary condition). Pass this diff to the real agent (with a real LLM call). Assert that at least one returned finding references the file and approximate line range of the injected defect and contains a non-empty description of the problem.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP: This invariant can only be violated across two distinct agentic steps — the inline-comment generation step and the summary-generation step. The summary is produced after inline comments are posted; a real failure is the LLM generating a summary that references findings it invented or hallucinated during the summary step, or the orchestration logic failing to carry the posted-findings set into the summary prompt. Neither a single-step Agentic test nor Integration (which would mock both steps) can catch this cross-step consistency failure. LLM-DEP: Both steps involve real LLM output; mocking either hides the real drift risk. REJECTION-GATE for Agentic: a single-step Agentic test cannot observe the relationship between two separately generated outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full agent pipeline against a PR with multiple seeded issues. After completion, collect all posted review comments (inline) and the top-level PR comment (summary) via the API. Parse the summary to extract each listed finding. Assert that for every finding in the summary there exists a corresponding inline comment (matched by file path and approximate line) posted in the same run, and that the total count of summary findings equals the total count of inline comments.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: 'A real pull request' is explicit — the behavior requires real GitHub API calls (read diff, create review comments, create PR comment). MULTI-STEP: Three distinct networked steps must all succeed in sequence: (1) authenticated diff fetch, (2) inline comment posting, (3) top-level comment posting. Failure can occur at any handoff: auth token scope insufficient for one action, rate limiting mid-sequence, diff format change breaking step 2's input, or step 3 referencing a review ID created in step 2. An in-process mock cannot surface these handoff failures. LLM-DEP: Real LLM calls are part of the pipeline. REJECTION-GATE for System: System covers a single real-infrastructure call; this behavior requires the entire three-step chain to succeed end-to-end.
behaviors.test_description: Point the agent at a real test GitHub repository with a pre-created PR containing a small deliberate bug. Invoke the automation with no human interaction. Assert: the diff-fetch HTTP call returns 200, at least one inline review comment is created on the PR (HTTP 201), and a top-level PR comment containing a non-empty summary is created (HTTP 201). Confirm all three artefacts are queryable via the GitHub API after the run.
